description_long: This bollard is a tall, rectangular column constructed from what appears to be white or light gray stone or concrete, featuring ornate floral relief patterns on its main vertical faces. The top of the bollard is capped with a stepped, circular design that adds a decorative touch, while the base is a solid, square platform providing stability. The surface is smooth with subtle matte finish, and the intricate relief work suggests a classical or ornamental style, suitable for upscale urban environments or historical districts. There are no visible openings, chains, or connectors, and the object appears to be a single, solid piece. The overall design is both functional as a barrier and visually appealing, blending utility with decorative urban architecture.
description: A tall, ornate stone or concrete bollard with floral relief patterns and a decorative stepped cap, set on a square base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the detailed floral relief and the full stepped cap design.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, with the same relief and cap visible.
description_view_2: Back view mirrors the front, displaying the same ornate relief and cap structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the identical relief and cap as other sides.
